name: Pons
location: Middle section of brainstem anterior to cerebellum and superior to medulla.
description: Bulging brainstem structure containing transverse pontine fibers connecting cerebrum to cerebellum. Houses nuclei for cranial nerves V, VI, VII, and VIII, and REM sleep generators.
bloodSupply: Pontine branches of the Basilar Artery
clinicalImportance: Inhibits spinal motor output during REM sleep so dreams are not physically enacted.
researchNotes: Locus coeruleus in the dorsal pons is the principal site of norepinephrine synthesis in the central nervous system.
interestingFacts: The word Pons means 'Bridge' in Latin.
